1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Orchestrates the hybrid simulator build: QEMU 9.x + vsim + TCG plugin.
3	# Run subcommands individually, or run with no args to build everything.
4	
5	set -euo pipefail
6	
7	ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
8	QEMU_DIR="$ROOT/hybrid_qemu"
9	VSIM_DIR="$ROOT/hybrid_vsim"
10	PLUGIN_DIR="$VSIM_DIR/hybrid/qemu_plugin"
11	QEMU_BUILD="$QEMU_DIR/build"
12	VSIM_BUILD="$VSIM_DIR/build"
13	
14	JOBS="$(nproc)"
15	
16	c_step()  { printf '\033[1;34m==>\033[0m \033[1m%s\033[0m\n' "$*"; }
17	c_ok()    { printf '\033[1;32m[ok]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
18	c_err()   { printf '\033[1;31m[err]\033[0m %s\n' "$*" >&2; }
19	
20	require_dir() {
21	  if [[ ! -d "$1" ]]; then
22	    c_err "missing $1 - did you run \`git submodule update --init --recursive\`?"
23	    exit 1
24	  fi
25	}
26	
27	build_qemu() {
28	  require_dir "$QEMU_DIR"
29	  c_step "QEMU: configure (if needed)"
30	  if [[ ! -f "$QEMU_BUILD/build.ninja" && ! -f "$QEMU_BUILD/Makefile" ]]; then
31	    mkdir -p "$QEMU_BUILD"
32	    (cd "$QEMU_BUILD" && ../configure \
33	      --target-list=riscv64-softmmu \
34	      --enable-plugins \
35	      --disable-werror)
36	  fi
37	  c_step "QEMU: build qemu-system-riscv64"
38	  if [[ -f "$QEMU_BUILD/build.ninja" ]]; then
39	    ninja -C "$QEMU_BUILD" qemu-system-riscv64
40	  else
41	    make -C "$QEMU_BUILD" -j"$JOBS" qemu-system-riscv64
42	  fi
43	  c_ok "QEMU built: $QEMU_BUILD/qemu-system-riscv64"
44	}
45	
46	build_vsim() {
47	  require_dir "$VSIM_DIR"
48	  c_step "vsim: configure (if needed)"
49	  if [[ ! -f "$VSIM_BUILD/CMakeCache.txt" ]]; then
50	    cmake -S "$VSIM_DIR" -B "$VSIM_BUILD" -G Ninja \
51	      -DVSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON \
52	      -DVSIM_QEMU_ROOT="$QEMU_DIR"
53	  fi
54	  c_step "vsim: build"
55	  cmake --build "$VSIM_BUILD" -j"$JOBS"
56	  c_ok "vsim built: $VSIM_BUILD/vsim"
57	}
58	
59	build_plugin() {
60	  require_dir "$PLUGIN_DIR"
61	  c_step "plugin: build libhybrid_handoff.so"
62	  make -C "$PLUGIN_DIR" QEMU_ROOT="$QEMU_DIR"
63	  c_ok "plugin built: $PLUGIN_DIR/libhybrid_handoff.so"
64	}
65	
66	run_tests() {
67	  require_dir "$VSIM_BUILD"
68	  c_step "ctest: hybrid-e2e label"
69	  (cd "$VSIM_BUILD" && ctest -L hybrid-e2e --output-on-failure)
70	}
71	
72	clean_all() {
73	  c_step "clean: remove build artifacts"
74	  rm -rf "$QEMU_BUILD" "$VSIM_BUILD"
75	  rm -f  "$PLUGIN_DIR/libhybrid_handoff.so"
76	  c_ok "cleaned"
77	}
78	
79	usage() {
80	  cat <<EOF
81	usage: $(basename "$0") [qemu|vsim|plugin|test|clean|all]
82	
83	  (no args)  same as 'all'
84	  qemu       configure + build hybrid_qemu/build/qemu-system-riscv64
85	  vsim       configure + build hybrid_vsim/build/vsim (VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON)
86	  plugin     build hybrid_vsim/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so
87	  test       ctest -L hybrid-e2e (in vsim build dir)
88	  clean      remove both build dirs and the plugin .so
89	  all        qemu, then vsim, then plugin (in this order)
90	EOF
91	}
92	
93	cmd="${1:-all}"
94	case "$cmd" in
95	  qemu)   build_qemu ;;
96	  vsim)   build_vsim ;;
97	  plugin) build_plugin ;;
98	  test)   run_tests ;;
99	  clean)  clean_all ;;
100	  all)    build_qemu; build_vsim; build_plugin ;;
101	  -h|--help|help) usage ;;
102	  *)      c_err "unknown command: $cmd"; usage; exit 2 ;;
103	esac
104

1	# hybrid_sim
2	
3	Workspace for the Andes hybrid simulator: QEMU 9.x (functional, MHz) + vsim
4	(SystemC + Verilator, cycle-accurate) sharing memory and architectural state
5	across a magic-CSR boundary.
6	
7	Two submodules:
8	
9	- `hybrid_qemu/` - QEMU fork hosting the TCG plugin
10	- `hybrid_vsim/` - vsim sources, including `hybrid/qemu_plugin/` (plugin source)
11	
12	## Quickstart
13	
14	```sh
15	git clone --recursive https://gitea.andestech.com/nick/hybrid_sim
16	cd hybrid_sim
17	./build.sh           # qemu, vsim, plugin (in that order)
18	./build.sh test      # ctest -L hybrid-e2e
19	```
20	
21	Subcommands: `qemu`, `vsim`, `plugin`, `test`, `clean`. Run `./build.sh -h`
22	for the full list.
23	
24	## Host dependencies
25	
26	- C/C++ toolchain (clang preferred), CMake >= 3.20, ninja
27	- Python 3
28	- glib-2.0 dev (for the QEMU plugin)
29	- pkg-config
30	- Verilator (auto-detected; set `VSIM_VERILATOR_EXECUTABLE` if needed)
31	- libraries QEMU itself needs (pixman, slirp, etc. - see `hybrid_qemu/README.rst`)
32	
33	## Outputs
34	
35	After `./build.sh`:
36	
37	- `hybrid_qemu/build/qemu-system-riscv64`
38	- `hybrid_vsim/build/vsim`
39	- `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so`

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# End-to-end roundtrip:
3	#   1. QEMU 1 + plugin: drains state at `csrwi 0x7C0, 0`.
4	#   2. vsim: resumes, runs body, drains at `csrwi 0x7C0, 1`, exits 200.
5	#   3. vsim's --qemu-binary path: spawns QEMU 2 with -gdb -S -semihosting,
6	#      restores GPRs+PC via gdbstub, continues.
7	#   4. QEMU 2 hits the RISC-V semihosting ebreak-triple, exits with the
8	#      semihosting exit_code (low 8 bits of a2 in the existing fixture).
9	#
10	# Fixture-parameterized:
11	#   FIXTURE     name of .elf under hybrid/test/  (default: handoff_roundtrip)
12	#   EXPECT_RC   expected vsim process exit code  (default: 3)
13	#
14	# Required environment:
15	#   VSIM_BIN         path to sim_<cpu> binary (host-runnable)
16	#   VSIM_QEMU        path to qemu-system-riscv64
17	#   VSIM_PLUGIN      path to libhybrid_handoff.so
18	#   VSIM_ELF         path to <fixture>.elf  (overrides FIXTURE if set)
19	
20	set -euo pipefail
21	
22	SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
23	REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)
24	
25	FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-handoff_roundtrip}"
26	EXPECT_RC="${EXPECT_RC:-3}"
27	
28	VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
29	VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
30	VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
31	VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/${FIXTURE}.elf}"
32	
33	for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
34	  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
35	    echo "skip: $f not built; run 'make' under hybrid/test and hybrid/qemu_plugin"
36	    exit 77   # CTest skip code when SKIP_RETURN_CODE is set
37	  fi
38	done
39	
40	STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
41	STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
42	SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
43	trap 'rm -f "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT
44	
45	echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3] QEMU 1: drain entry state -> $STATE_QEMU"
46	"$VSIM_QEMU" -M virt -bios none -nographic \
47	  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
48	  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_QEMU" >/dev/null 2>&1
49	
50	if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
51	  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
52	  exit 1
53	fi
54	
55	echo "[$FIXTURE 2/3] vsim: resume + drain + spawn QEMU 2"
56	set +e
57	timeout 60 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
58	  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
59	  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
60	  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
61	RC=$?
62	set -e
63	
64	if [ "$RC" != "$EXPECT_RC" ]; then
65	  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: expected exit $EXPECT_RC, got $RC"
66	  echo "--- sim log ---"
67	  cat "$SIM_LOG"
68	  exit 1
69	fi
70	
71	echo "[$FIXTURE 3/3] PASS: round-trip exit code $RC"
72

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Negative test: a corrupted --resume-from-qemu state file must trigger a
3	# fail-fast in vsim with a recognizable error message, not a segfault, hang,
4	# or silent forward run on garbage state.
5	
6	set -euo pipefail
7	
8	SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
9	REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)
10	
11	VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
12	VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/handoff_roundtrip.elf}"
13	
14	for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
15	  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
16	    echo "skip: $f not built"
17	    exit 77
18	  fi
19	done
20	
21	BAD_STATE=$(mktemp)
22	SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
23	trap 'rm -f "$BAD_STATE" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT
24	
25	# 32 bytes of zeros: shorter than hybrid_state_v1, hits the short-read branch.
26	dd if=/dev/zero of="$BAD_STATE" bs=32 count=1 status=none
27	
28	set +e
29	timeout 10 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
30	  --resume-from-qemu "$BAD_STATE" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
31	RC=$?
32	set -e
33	
34	# 0    - silent pass on garbage (BAD)
35	# 124  - bash timeout fired -> vsim hung (BAD)
36	# 200  - hybrid handoff exit (BAD: drain ran, state was treated as valid)
37	# else - any non-zero error path (OK)
38	if [ "$RC" -eq 0 ] || [ "$RC" -eq 200 ] || [ "$RC" -eq 124 ]; then
39	  echo "FAIL[rt_corrupt_state]: expected non-zero, non-200, non-timeout exit; got $RC"
40	  echo "--- sim log ---"
41	  cat "$SIM_LOG"
42	  exit 1
43	fi
44	
45	if ! grep -Eq "short read|bad magic|cannot open" "$SIM_LOG"; then
46	  echo "FAIL[rt_corrupt_state]: stderr did not match 'short read|bad magic|cannot open'"
47	  echo "--- sim log ---"
48	  cat "$SIM_LOG"
49	  exit 1
50	fi
51	
52	echo "[rt_corrupt_state] PASS: vsim fail-fast with rc=$RC"
53

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Negative test: --qemu-binary points at a missing file. Vsim's drain must
3	# still fire and the spawn-fail must propagate as exit 127 (the standard
4	# exec-failure code), not as a 5 s connect_loopback timeout that masquerades
5	# as something else, and not as a hang that ties up CTest.

7	set -euo pipefail

10	REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

25	STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)

28	
29	# Phase A: real QEMU 1 produces a valid entry-state snapshot so vsim has

65	  echo "FAIL[rt_no_qemu_binary]: background jobs still running"
66	  exit 1
67	fi

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Tier-2 round-trip e2e: cross-simulator memory survival via shared mmap.
3	#
4	# QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2 all map the same backing file. Phase A writes a
5	# pattern in QEMU 1; Phase B verifies + mutates in vsim; Phase C re-reads
6	# in QEMU 2. Memory must survive both handoffs.
7	#
8	# Required environment:
9	#   VSIM_BIN     path to sim_<cpu> binary (host-runnable)
10	#   VSIM_QEMU    path to qemu-system-riscv64
11	#   VSIM_PLUGIN  path to libhybrid_handoff.so
12	
13	set -euo pipefail
14	
15	SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
16	REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)
17	
18	FIXTURE=rt_shared_mem
19	EXPECT_RC=0
20	
21	VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
22	VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
23	VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
24	VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/${FIXTURE}.elf}"
25	
26	for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
27	  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
28	    echo "skip: $f not built; run 'make' under hybrid/test and hybrid/qemu_plugin"
29	    exit 77
30	  fi
31	done
32	
33	# 128 MB matches the QEMU virt machine default; vsim must use the same size.
34	SHARED_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
35	SHARED_MEM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-rt-shared.XXXXXX)
36	STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
37	STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
38	SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
39	trap 'rm -f "$SHARED_MEM" "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT
40	
41	truncate -s "$SHARED_SIZE" "$SHARED_MEM"
42
